datasets.name: aircraft_tracking_grouped
datasets.description: Real-time global aircraft positions from OpenSky ADS-B receivers, published as longer transparent VP9-alpha DASH chunks. This grouped rendition reduces tiny per-chunk bitrate spikes while the low-latency Aircraft Traffic stream remains available for fresher updates.

datasets.name: ais_traffic_grouped
datasets.description: Real-time global AIS vessel positions sampled every 10 minutes and published as longer transparent VP9-alpha DASH chunks. This grouped rendition reduces tiny per-chunk bitrate spikes while the low-latency AIS stream remains available for fresher updates.

datasets.name: aod550_forecast
datasets.description: 5-day global aerosol optical depth forecast at 550 nm from the Copernicus Atmosphere Monitoring Service (CAMS). Shows predicted evolution of dust storms, wildfire smoke plumes, and pollution events. 3-hourly timesteps (41 frames) from the latest 00Z forecast run. 0.4° resolution with transparent overlay.

datasets.name: aod550_grouped
datasets.description: Global aerosol optical depth at 550nm from the Copernicus Atmosphere Monitoring Service (CAMS), published as longer transparent VP9-alpha DASH chunks. This grouped stream reduces tiny per-chunk bitrate spikes for DASH players while the low-latency Aerosol Optical Depth stream carries the freshest updates.

datasets.name: black_carbon_sources_grouped
datasets.description: Near-real-time black carbon emission source diagnostics from NASA GEOS-FP / GOCART-2G. Anthropogenic black carbon emissions are rendered in cyan-blue and biomass-burning black carbon emissions in orange-red; overlap trends toward white. This is a model/assimilation emissions diagnostic, not a direct satellite observation. 3-hourly GEOS-FP extended aerosol diagnostics with typical sub-day latency.

datasets.name: ch4_grouped
datasets.description: Global column-mean methane molar fraction from the Copernicus Atmosphere Monitoring Service (CAMS), published as longer transparent VP9-alpha DASH chunks. This grouped stream reduces tiny per-chunk bitrate spikes for DASH players while the low-latency Methane stream carries the freshest updates.

datasets.name: chlorophyll
datasets.description: Monthly global ocean chlorophyll-a concentration from satellite ocean-color sensors. Indicates phytoplankton abundance — green/yellow areas show productive waters, blue areas show oligotrophic open ocean. Source: NOAA Science On a Sphere real-time feed.

datasets.name: city_air_quality
datasets.description: Real-time Air Quality Index (AQI) for cities worldwide from the World Air Quality Index project (aqicn.org). Stations are aggregated to a 0.5° grid showing the worst reading per area. Color-coded by EPA AQI categories: green (good, 0-50), yellow (moderate, 51-100), orange (unhealthy for sensitive groups, 101-150), red (unhealthy, 151-200), purple (very unhealthy, 201-300), maroon (hazardous, 301+). Updated every 6 hours.

datasets.name: city_air_quality_equi
datasets.description: Real-time Air Quality Index (AQI) for cities worldwide, with equirectangular compensation for spherical display projection. Circles are horizontally stretched by 1/cos(latitude) so they appear round on a sphere. Same data as City Air Quality.

datasets.name: clms_snow_cover_grouped
datasets.description: Daily near-real-time global snow cover extent from the Copernicus Land Monitoring Service, derived from VIIRS observations at 1 km resolution. Snow-covered pixels are shown as a transparent-to-white overlay; snow-free and no-data areas are transparent.

datasets.name: clms_soil_water_index_grouped
datasets.description: Daily near-real-time global Soil Water Index from the Copernicus Land Monitoring Service, derived from MetOp ASCAT scatterometer observations at 12.5 km resolution. Higher values indicate wetter soils; no-data and water areas are transparent.

datasets.name: clouds_grouped
datasets.description: Real-time global cloud cover composite from geostationary weather satellites, published as longer transparent VP9-alpha DASH chunks. This grouped rendition targets 4-6 second playable chunks without re-encoding while the low-latency Global Cloud Cover stream remains available for fresher updates.

datasets.name: coral_bleaching
datasets.description: Daily global coral bleaching alert from NOAA Coral Reef Watch. Shows 7-day maximum bleaching heat stress levels — from No Stress (blue) through Watch, Warning, Alert Level 1 and Alert Level 2 (red). 4096x2048 RGBA with alpha transparency. ~3-day latency.

datasets.name: drought
datasets.description: Weekly US Drought Monitor classification from D0 (abnormally dry) to D4 (exceptional drought). A partnership between NOAA, USDA, and the National Drought Mitigation Center. Source: NOAA Science On a Sphere real-time feed.

datasets.name: ecmwf_t2m_forecast
datasets.description: 72-hour global 2-meter air temperature forecast from ECMWF IFS Open Data at 0.25 degree resolution. Three-hour timesteps show expected near-surface temperature in degrees Celsius using the turbo colormap. Replaced four times daily.

datasets.name: fires
datasets.description: Daily global active fire detections from VIIRS and MODIS satellite instruments, showing wildfire and agricultural burning activity. Source: NOAA Science On a Sphere real-time feed.

datasets.name: firms_fires
datasets.description: Daily global active fire detections from NASA's Fire Information for Resource Management System (FIRMS). Uses VIIRS and MODIS instruments to detect thermal anomalies associated with wildfires, agricultural burns, and industrial flares.

datasets.name: gdacs_disasters
datasets.description: Active global disaster events from the Global Disaster Alerting Coordination System (UN/EC). Tracks earthquakes, floods, tropical cyclones, wildfires, volcanic eruptions, and droughts worldwide. Color-coded by severity: Green (advisory), Orange (watch), Red (alert). Updated every 6 minutes.

datasets.name: gdelt_events_grouped
datasets.description: Global political events from the GDELT Project, compiled from news sources worldwide every 15 minutes. Shows cooperation (blue) and conflict (red) events geocoded to specific locations. Covers protests, diplomatic exchanges, military actions, and more. ~73,000 events per day.

datasets.name: geocolor
datasets.description: Near-global true-color/infrared composite from 5 geostationary satellites: GOES-East (19), GOES-West (18), Meteosat-12, Meteosat-9, and Himawari-9. Daytime shows true color; nighttime shows IR cloud composite with city lights. Updated every 10 minutes via CIRA SLIDER.

datasets.name: gfs_precip_forecast
datasets.description: 72-hour global precipitation rate forecast from NOAA GFS 0.25 degree operational forecasts. Three-hour timesteps show expected rain and snow intensity as a transparent overlay, with heavier precipitation shown in brighter blues. Replaced four times daily.

datasets.name: gfs_wind_10m_grouped
datasets.description: Near-real-time global 10 m wind speed and direction from NOAA GFS 0.25 degree operational forecasts. Uses a short forecast lead from the latest available cycle for much lower latency than ERA5, rendered as transparent wind arrows colored by speed.

datasets.name: gfw_traffic
datasets.description: Hourly global marine vessel traffic from Global Fishing Watch (AIS vessel presence, ~96-hour latency). Each vessel grid cell is drawn as a distinct dot coloured by the dominant continent of its flag-state vessel presence — Africa, Asia, Europe, North America, South America and Oceania — so shipping lanes and ports resolve into individual cells rather than a density blur. Published as newest-anchored grouped VP9-alpha DASH chunks. (GFW's gridded presence report exposes flag and counts but not vessel type, so cells are coloured by dominant flag continent rather than ship category.)

datasets.name: glm_lightning_grouped
datasets.description: Near-global lightning detections combining GOES GLM, EUMETSAT MTG Lightning Imager, and Blitzortung gap-fill, published as longer transparent VP9-alpha DASH chunks. This grouped rendition reduces tiny per-chunk bitrate spikes while the low-latency Lightning stream remains available for fresher updates.

datasets.name: imerg_precip_nrt_grouped
datasets.description: Near-real-time global precipitation rate from NASA GPM IMERG, fetched from NASA GIBS 30-minute imagery and published as longer transparent VP9-alpha DASH chunks. Rain and snow intensity are shown as a transparent overlay so dry areas reveal the base globe. This grouped stream reduces tiny per-chunk bitrate spikes for DASH players.

datasets.name: knmi_precip_forecast
datasets.description: KNMI HARMONIE-AROME 2 km hourly precipitation accumulation forecast over the Netherlands and surrounding area, out to ~2.5 days ahead. Regional WMS imagery georeferenced by latitude/longitude bounds; refreshed twice daily.

datasets.name: knmi_precip_rtcor_grouped
datasets.description: Near-real-time 5-minute radar/gauge precipitation accumulations over the Netherlands and surrounding area from KNMI, published as longer transparent VP9-alpha DASH chunks. This grouped rendition reduces tiny per-chunk bitrate spikes while preserving regional DSA georeferencing.

datasets.name: knmi_temp_forecast
datasets.description: KNMI HARMONIE-AROME 2 km hourly 2-metre air temperature forecast over the Netherlands and surrounding area, out to ~2.5 days ahead. Regional WMS imagery georeferenced by latitude/longitude bounds; refreshed twice daily.

datasets.name: knmi_temp_rtcor_grouped
datasets.description: Near-real-time 2-metre air temperature over the Netherlands from the KNMI HARMONIE-AROME 2 km model, published as longer transparent VP9-alpha DASH chunks. Hourly nowcast, regional WMS imagery georeferenced by latitude/longitude bounds.

datasets.name: knmi_wind_forecast
datasets.description: KNMI HARMONIE-AROME 2 km hourly near-surface wind forecast over the Netherlands and surrounding area, out to ~2.5 days ahead, rendered as wind barbs coloured by speed (knots). Regional WMS imagery georeferenced by latitude/longitude bounds; refreshed twice daily.

datasets.name: knmi_wind_rtcor_grouped
datasets.description: Near-real-time near-surface wind over the Netherlands from the KNMI HARMONIE-AROME 2 km model, rendered as wind barbs coloured by speed (knots) and published as longer transparent VP9-alpha DASH chunks. Hourly nowcast, regional WMS imagery georeferenced by latitude/longitude bounds.

datasets.name: land_surface_temp_grouped
datasets.description: Near-real-time global land surface temperature from the Copernicus Land Monitoring Service, derived from geostationary thermal infrared observations at ~5 km resolution and published as longer transparent VP9-alpha DASH chunks. Land areas are shown as a transparent overlay so oceans and no-data regions reveal the base globe. This grouped stream reduces tiny per-chunk bitrate spikes for DASH players while the low-latency Land Surface Temperature stream carries the freshest hourly updates.

datasets.name: marine_heatwaves_grouped
datasets.description: Daily global marine heatwave detection using Hobday et al. (2018) categorization. Compares Copernicus Marine OSTIA SST against 1991-2020 NOAA OISST climatology. Categories: Moderate (yellow), Strong (orange), Severe (red), Extreme (dark red). Transparent overlay at 0.25° resolution.

datasets.name: modis
datasets.description: Daily global true-color imagery from NASA's MODIS (Moderate Resolution Imaging Spectroradiometer) instrument aboard the Terra and Aqua satellites. Source: NOAA Science On a Sphere real-time feed.

datasets.name: no2_grouped
datasets.description: Global surface-level nitrogen dioxide from NASA GEOS-CF atmospheric composition model, published as longer transparent VP9-alpha DASH chunks. NO2 is a traffic- and combustion-related pollutant and respiratory irritant. This grouped stream reduces tiny per-chunk bitrate spikes while the low-latency Nitrogen Dioxide stream carries the freshest updates.

datasets.name: noaa_precip_forecast
datasets.description: NOAA National Digital Forecast Database (NDFD) 6-hour quantitative precipitation forecast over the contiguous United States, out to ~2.5 days. Official NWS gridded forecast served as transparent WMS imagery; refreshed twice daily.

datasets.name: noaa_precip_rtcor_grouped
datasets.description: NOAA MRMS 1-hour radar precipitation estimate over the contiguous United States, observed mosaic updated hourly, published as longer transparent VP9-alpha DASH chunks.

datasets.name: noaa_temp_forecast
datasets.description: NOAA National Digital Forecast Database (NDFD) surface air temperature forecast over the contiguous United States, hourly out to ~2.5 days. Official NWS gridded forecast served as transparent WMS imagery; refreshed twice daily.

datasets.name: noaa_temp_outlook_7day
datasets.description: NOAA National Digital Forecast Database (NDFD) daily maximum temperature outlook over the contiguous United States, out to 7 days. Official NWS gridded forecast served as transparent WMS imagery; refreshed twice daily.

datasets.name: noaa_temp_rtcor_grouped
datasets.description: NOAA NDFD surface air temperature over the contiguous United States, current-hour nowcast updated hourly, published as longer transparent VP9-alpha DASH chunks.

datasets.name: noaa_wind_forecast
datasets.description: NOAA National Digital Forecast Database (NDFD) sustained wind speed forecast over the contiguous United States, hourly out to ~2.5 days. Official NWS gridded forecast served as transparent WMS imagery; refreshed twice daily.

datasets.name: noaa_wind_rtcor_grouped
datasets.description: NOAA NDFD sustained wind speed over the contiguous United States, current-hour nowcast updated hourly, published as longer transparent VP9-alpha DASH chunks.

datasets.name: ocean_currents
datasets.description: Daily global ocean surface currents and temperature from the Copernicus Marine Global Ocean Physics Analysis and Forecast system at 0.083 degree (~8 km). Animated particle flow shows current direction and speed over a rainbow temperature background (-2 to 32 C). Updated daily.

datasets.name: ocean_currents_vangogh
datasets.description: Global ocean surface currents visualized as dense flowing particle trails in the style of van Gogh's Starry Night. Swirling indigo-cyan-gold trails over a dark sea surface temperature background. CMEMS GLO12 at 0.083° resolution.

datasets.name: ocean_temp_forecast
datasets.description: 7-day global ocean surface temperature forecast from the Copernicus Marine Global Ocean Physics Analysis and Forecast system. Daily timesteps show forecast sea temperature at the surface, colored from cold blues to warm reds. Replaced daily.

datasets.name: ostia_sst_nrt_grouped
datasets.description: Daily near-real-time global sea surface temperature from the UK Met Office OSTIA L4 product via Copernicus Marine. Ocean pixels are rendered as a transparent temperature overlay; land and missing data are transparent.

datasets.name: pm25_grouped
datasets.description: Global surface PM2.5 fine particulate matter from NASA GEOS-CF atmospheric composition model, published as longer transparent VP9-alpha DASH chunks. PM2.5 is the air pollutant most strongly associated with health impacts. This grouped stream reduces tiny per-chunk bitrate spikes while the low-latency Air Quality stream carries the freshest updates.

datasets.name: quakes
datasets.description: Daily global earthquake activity showing recent seismic events. Point sizes indicate magnitude, colors indicate depth. Source: NOAA Science On a Sphere real-time feed (USGS data).

datasets.name: sea_level_forecast
datasets.description: 7-day global sea level forecast from the Copernicus Marine Global Ocean Physics Analysis and Forecast system. 168 hourly timesteps showing total sea level including tides, storm surge, and ocean dynamics. Uses the same blue-to-red jet color scale as the STOFS sea level forecast, with transparent land. Replaced daily.

datasets.name: si10_grouped
datasets.description: Global 10m wind speed from ERA5 reanalysis by ECMWF, published as longer transparent VP9-alpha DASH chunks over a transparent overlay. This grouped stream reduces tiny per-chunk bitrate spikes for DASH players while the low-latency 10m Wind Speed stream carries the freshest updates.

datasets.name: smn_precip_forecast
datasets.description: Mexico's Servicio Meteorológico Nacional (CONAGUA) daily precipitation forecast by municipality (~2,460 municipalities), out to ~4 days. Each municipality is a point coloured by forecast precipitation amount (mm) in a regional Mexico bounding box. Refreshed twice daily.

datasets.name: smn_temp_forecast
datasets.description: Mexico's Servicio Meteorológico Nacional (CONAGUA) daily maximum-temperature forecast by municipality (~2,460 municipalities), out to ~4 days. Each municipality is a point coloured by forecast high temperature in a regional Mexico bounding box. Refreshed twice daily.

datasets.name: smn_wind_forecast
datasets.description: Mexico's Servicio Meteorológico Nacional (CONAGUA) daily wind-speed forecast by municipality (~2,460 municipalities), out to ~4 days. Each municipality is a point coloured by forecast wind speed (km/h) in a regional Mexico bounding box. Refreshed twice daily.

datasets.name: snow_ice
datasets.description: Daily global snow and ice coverage derived from satellite observations. Shows the extent of snow cover on land and sea-ice in polar regions. Source: NOAA Science On a Sphere real-time feed.

datasets.name: so2_grouped
datasets.description: Global total column sulphur dioxide from the Copernicus Atmosphere Monitoring Service (CAMS), published as longer transparent VP9-alpha DASH chunks. This grouped stream reduces tiny per-chunk bitrate spikes for DASH players while the low-latency Sulphur Dioxide stream carries the freshest updates.

datasets.name: sst_anomaly
datasets.description: Daily global sea surface temperature anomaly from NOAA NESDIS. Shows departures from the long-term average — warm anomalies (red) and cold anomalies (blue) — useful for tracking El Nino/La Nina patterns. Source: NOAA Science On a Sphere.

datasets.name: sst_grouped
datasets.description: Global sea surface temperature from ERA5 reanalysis by ECMWF, published as longer transparent VP9-alpha DASH chunks. Land and no-data areas are transparent so it reads as an ocean overlay. This grouped stream reduces tiny per-chunk bitrate spikes for DASH players while the low-latency Sea Surface Temperature stream carries the freshest updates.

datasets.name: stofs_sea_level
datasets.description: 7-day global sea level forecast from the STOFS-2D-Global shadow system (ADCIRC model) operated by Notre Dame's Computational Hydraulics Laboratory. 168 hourly frames showing combined water level (storm surge + astronomical tides). Jet colormap from -1.5m (blue) to +1.5m (red) with transparent land. Replaced daily.

datasets.name: sun_chromosphere_304_grouped
datasets.description: Near-real-time SDO/AIA 304 Å extreme-ultraviolet view of the Sun's lower atmosphere, emphasizing the fiery-looking chromosphere, transition region, prominences, and active regions. The Earth-facing full disk is reprojected to an equirectangular map and mirror-wrapped onto a sphere. Published as longer VP9 DASH chunks; the low-latency source carries the freshest 30-minute updates.

datasets.name: sun_corona_171_grouped
datasets.description: Near-real-time SDO/AIA 171 Å extreme-ultraviolet view of the solar corona, highlighting plasma around 600,000 K and the bright loop structures that trace the Sun's magnetic field. The Earth-facing full disk is reprojected to an equirectangular map and mirror-wrapped onto a sphere. Published as longer VP9 DASH chunks; the low-latency source carries the freshest 30-minute updates.

datasets.name: sun_surface_grouped
datasets.description: Near-real-time view of the Sun's surface (photosphere) from NASA's Solar Dynamics Observatory HMI white-light continuum, showing sunspots. The Earth-facing full disk is reprojected to an equirectangular map and mirror-wrapped onto a sphere — no live imagery exists for the Sun's far side. Limb darkening is flattened so the Sun reads as a clean rotating globe. Published as longer VP9 DASH chunks; the low-latency source carries the freshest 30-minute updates.

datasets.name: surface_o3_grouped
datasets.description: Global surface-level ozone concentration from NASA GEOS-CF atmospheric composition model, published as longer transparent VP9-alpha DASH chunks. Ground-level ozone is a respiratory irritant formed by sunlight reacting with precursor pollutants. This grouped stream reduces tiny per-chunk bitrate spikes while preserving the low-latency source stream.

datasets.name: t2m_grouped
datasets.description: Global 2-meter air temperature from ERA5 reanalysis by ECMWF, published as longer DASH chunks for efficient playback. Hourly snapshots at 0.25° (~31 km), colored -60 °C (deep blue) to +40 °C (deep red) with the turbo colormap.

datasets.name: tcc_grouped
datasets.description: Global total cloud cover from ERA5 reanalysis by ECMWF, published as longer transparent VP9-alpha DASH chunks. Clear-sky areas are transparent so the base globe shows through. This grouped stream reduces tiny per-chunk bitrate spikes for DASH players while the low-latency Total Cloud Cover stream carries the freshest updates.

datasets.name: tp_grouped
datasets.description: Global total precipitation from ERA5 reanalysis by ECMWF, published as longer transparent VP9-alpha DASH chunks. Dry areas are transparent so the base globe shows through. This grouped stream reduces tiny per-chunk bitrate spikes for DASH players while the low-latency Total Precipitation stream carries the freshest updates.

datasets.name: tropomi_no2_grouped
datasets.description: Tropospheric nitrogen dioxide column observed by the TROPOMI instrument on ESA's Sentinel-5P satellite. Shipping lanes, cities, highways, power plants, and industrial zones light up vividly against the clean ocean and rural background. Unlike model data, this is direct measurement from space at 5.5 x 3.5 km native resolution, composited daily onto a 0.25° grid. Quality-filtered (qa > 0.75) to remove cloud-contaminated pixels.

datasets.name: utci_grouped
datasets.description: Universal Thermal Climate Index from ERA5-HEAT by ECMWF — the felt temperature combining air temperature, humidity, wind, and radiation — published as longer DASH chunks for efficient playback. Hourly at 0.25° (~31 km), colored by thermal-stress category from extreme cold to extreme heat stress.

datasets.name: uv_index
datasets.description: 5-day global UV index forecast from the Copernicus Atmosphere Monitoring Service (CAMS). Hourly timesteps showing biologically effective UV dose at the surface. Higher values mean greater sunburn and skin cancer risk. Rendered with the WHO/WMO 5-band UV Index scale: Low 0-2 (green), Moderate 3-5 (yellow), High 6-7 (orange), Very High 8-10 (red), Extreme 11+ (violet). 0.4° resolution, replaced daily.

datasets.name: vegetation
datasets.description: Weekly global Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) showing plant health and greenness. Green areas indicate healthy vegetation, brown/yellow areas indicate sparse or stressed vegetation. Source: NOAA Science On a Sphere real-time feed.

datasets.name: viirs_earth_view_grouped
datasets.description: Daily satellite view from the VIIRS Day/Night Band on Suomi NPP. Shows the entire Earth from a polar orbit — cloud patterns, the day-night terminator, and city lights visible on the nighttime side. Grayscale composite at 1 km resolution. Source: NASA GIBS.

datasets.name: wind_vangogh
datasets.description: Global wind speed and direction at 10 m visualized as dense flowing particle trails in the style of van Gogh's Starry Night. Swirling indigo-cyan-gold trails over a dark Blue Marble background. ERA5 reanalysis at 0.25° resolution.

datasets.name: wind_vangogh_overlay
datasets.description: Global 10m wind visualized as flowing van Gogh-style particle trails on a transparent background, for compositing over a globe or basemap. Shares the ERA5 10m wind data with the Wind (Van Gogh) showcase; here the trail coverage drives the alpha channel so there is no Blue Marble backdrop. 0.25° resolution.
